---
title: "Boundary And Cobordism"
---

Boundary/cobordism language starts here with directed finite intervals. The current lesson is about orientation and endpoints: before the project can discuss cobordism-style structure, it needs checked finite boundary facts.

::: {.warning-box}
The current theorem cards certify directed-boundary seeds only. A full cobordism category, manifold theory, and field-theory-style boundary calculus remain future work.
:::

## What To Notice

- A directed interval has an input side and an output side.
- Boundary facts are finite and explicit in the current model.
- Future cobordism language must preserve these orientation guardrails.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

Why is endpoint orientation a useful first proof target for later boundary and cobordism language?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BC-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BC-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BC-T0003"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BC-T0004"></div>

<p data-paper-ref="papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_04_BOUNDARY_COBORDISM_CALCULUS.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_04_BOUNDARY_COBORDISM_CALCULUS.md">Boundary Cobordism Calculus</a></p>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
